task_description: Build headless WordPress Astro site herbertokolowitz.com
reflection_q1: Should have extracted Language type and SUPPORTED_LOCALES constant from the start — the getStaticPaths sync trap was predictable
reflection_q2: Front-load a shared i18n constants file for any multilingual project; generate shared layout components to avoid index.astro duplication
reflection_q3: Council skill in THINK phase would have been valuable for debating WP URL/API strategy; FirstPrinciples for the i18n architecture

task_description: Archive Astro branch, restore clean static main
reflection_q1: The manual git checkout -- revert step was unnecessary — switching branches auto-restores tracked files. Could have skipped 3 planned commands.
reflection_q2: Test SSH auth earlier (THINK phase as prerequisite) rather than BUILD. Would catch auth issues before any destructive work begins.
reflection_q3: No capabilities were needed here; this was pure git operations. Correct call to skip capability selection for a plan-execution-only task.

task_description: Implement ATOM Obsidian second brain integration
reflection_q1: Could have used prd-utils.ts exports for findLatestPRD and parseFrontmatter — chose self-contained standalone script instead to avoid cross-directory coupling
reflection_q2: Running /simplify mid-EXECUTE rather than post-EXECUTE would catch import and efficiency issues earlier, reducing rework
reflection_q3: prd-utils.ts already had all the PRD parsing I needed — should scan hooks/lib/ for reusable utilities at the start of any tool-writing task

task_description: Add Noller AI Zotero PDFs to NotebookLM
reflection_q1: Could have run test upload earlier to validate auth before full OBSERVE. Worked fine but an early smoke test would be cleaner.
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would pre-validate the notebooklm library version compatibility (sources bug with s.type) before trusting the CLI verify step.
reflection_q3: No additional capabilities needed for this task — it was purely mechanical upload work. NotebookLM skill was the right sole capability.

task_description: Create Zotero collection with 24 philosophy bibliography items
reflection_q1: Nothing different — clear plan, clean execution, all 8 ISC passed first run
reflection_q2: Pre-check for existing collection name to avoid duplicates on re-run
reflection_q3: No additional capabilities needed for this scripting+API task

task_description: Attach Google Drive PDFs to Zotero entries via API
reflection_q1: Should have tested one gws download before building full loop — saved 10min of debugging
reflection_q2: Test a single API/CLI command with one sample item before writing the full batch script
reflection_q3: No additional capabilities needed for this scripting task

task_description: Improve TH Ulm Kursvorstellung file with Telos/Zeitplan
reflection_q1: Fetched Google Sheets directly — correct, as Zeitplan had different semester dates than Syllabus.md. Backup created before any edits.
reflection_q2: Could have auto-detected gws flag format from --help first to avoid one retry.
reflection_q3: No additional skills needed — this was pure content/text work, document editing was sufficient.

task_description: Review WLAN map devices improve performance
reflection_q1: Should have skipped per-IP port scanning loop — avahi provided richer data faster
reflection_q2: Smarter approach: avahi first, then targeted probing of unknowns only
reflection_q3: No PAI skills needed for this — pure bash network tooling was the right approach

reflection_q1: No nmap made service identification harder - banner grabs were mostly empty for IoT devices which firewall everything
reflection_q2: Should have checked ZTE F680 CVE database via WebSearch for specific known exploits
reflection_q3: WebAssessment skill would have structured the router assessment better but standard bash was sufficient

task_description: Plan Claude PAI parity on IdeaPad notebook
reflection_q1: Existing replicate-to-laptop.sh was a great starting point - should read it before planning to avoid redundancy
reflection_q2: Could have diffed hook/skill lists programmatically instead of manually comparing ls outputs
reflection_q3: No PAI skills needed - pure inventory + comparison was the right approach

task_description: Execute PAI parity migration to IdeaPad notebook
reflection_q1: Sudo issue with npm required workaround - should check install method before planning update step
reflection_q2: The replicate script + targeted rsync combo worked cleanly - good pattern for future migrations
reflection_q3: No special PAI skills needed - pure bash/rsync execution

task_description: Update SetPieceTakers Notion space with 2026 data
reflection_q1: Should have fetched all page contents before update_content calls to avoid empty-block match failures
reflection_q2: Pre-fetch every target page before update_content — add this as a standard step before any Notion write wave
reflection_q3: Background Agents were listed but not spawned via Task tool — did all work directly which was faster given Notion MCP was available in main context
